FRONTIER.Definition: the edge of an area where people were settling and building towns

FUGITIVES.Definition: people running away from the law

FORGOTTEN TO HISTORY.Definition: no longer remembered by most people

FIT OF RAGE.Definition: a sudden burst of extreme anger

GRAZING.Definition: lightly touching and causing a small injury

HUNTING GROUND.Definition: an area where he regularly looked for something or someone

ODDS ARE STACKED AGAINST HIM.Definition: he has very little chance of success

STANDOFF.Definition: a tense situation where neither side gave in

TAKEN INTO CUSTODY.Definition: arrested and kept by the authorities

SENTENCED.Definition: officially given a punishment by a court

LEGISLATOR.Definition: a person who helped make laws

BUTLER.Definition: a servant who managed a large house

FIELD LABOURER.Definition: a worker who worked on a farm

CONFEDERATE.Definition: connected to the southern states during the American Civil War

DEATH SENTENCE.Definition: a court decision that he would be executed

MARCHED.Definition: made to walk in a group

AT GUNPOINT.Definition: while being threatened with a gun

GHETTO.Definition: an area where a group of people was forced to live apart from others

LIKE THE BACK OF HIS HAND.Definition: very well and in great detail

FEUDS.Definition: long and bitter arguments between people or groups

HELD GRUDGES.Definition: stayed angry about something for a long time

ABOLITION.Definition: the ending of it by law

MINDING HIS BUSINESS.Definition: quietly focusing on his own life

STICKLER.Definition: a person who strongly insisted on rules

MORALISTIC.Definition: strongly focused on what was right and wrong

RIGHTEOUSNESS.Definition: the belief that he was morally right

PURSUIT.Definition: the act of trying to achieve it

WORLDVIEW.Definition: his way of seeing and understanding the world

DEPUTIES.Definition: officers who helped a senior law officer

LAW ENFORCEMENT.Definition: the work of making sure people obeyed the law

APPOINTED.Definition: officially given the job or position

OVERSTATEMENT.Definition: a statement that makes it seem bigger or more important than it was

OUTLAWS.Definition: criminals who lived outside the law

OUTLAW TERRITORY.Definition: an area controlled or used by criminals

SADDLE.Definition: the seat placed on the horse for riding

CAMPFIRE.Definition: a fire made outdoors for warmth or cooking

HANDCUFFS.Definition: metal rings used to lock a person's wrists together

DRIFTER.Definition: a person who moved from place to place without a permanent home or job

DEADLY QUICK.Definition: extremely fast, especially in a dangerous situation

EXAGGERATION.Definition: something described as bigger or better than it really was

PISTOL.Definition: a small gun held in one hand

WARRANT.Definition: an official document allowing them to arrest someone

ALOUD.Definition: in a voice that could be heard

SATCHEL.Definition: a bag carried over the shoulder

MIRACULOUSLY.Definition: in a surprising way that seems impossible

ACCEPT HIS FATE.Definition: accept what was going to happen

BY ALL ACCOUNTS.Definition: according to everyone who knows about it

MODEL CITIZEN.Definition: a person who behaved very responsibly

PATROLLED.Definition: travelled around to keep them safe

OBITUARY.Definition: a newspaper article about his life after he died

FIT THE MOULD.Definition: match the usual type or pattern

MASKED.Definition: wearing something that covered the face

CRACK SHOT.Definition: a person who shot very accurately

PROTAGONIST.Definition: the main character in the story

HEROIC AGENCY.Definition: the ability to take brave action and make things happen

CONVENIENTLY.Definition: in a way that was easy or useful

BENT THE RULES.Definition: did not follow the rules exactly
